168: Proposal — v1.0.0: Recovery, Crash-Injection & Soak Testing (`recovery-testing`)
172: Requirement: Checkpoint sequence numbers are gapless, monotonic, and scoped per wallet+network
173: Requirement: load and history distinguish "no checkpoint" from "checkpoint exists elsewhere"
175: GC architecture and query-tracing: research findings
177: Requirement: load always fully verifies chunk integrity before returning
178: Requirement: prune rejects a retainCount that is not a positive safe integer, before any deletion runs
180: Requirement: A chunk still referenced by any surviving manifest is never reclaimed (Law C2a)
181: Requirement: A manifest preserves the exact order and multiplicity of its chunks, including repeats
182: Requirement: An already-aborted opts.signal rejects before any database work; a signal aborting after the call has begun has no effect
183: Requirement: Checkpoint payloads are split into fixed-size chunks with a correctly-sized remainder
184: Requirement: CheckpointSummary metadata is populated and label round-trips
185: Requirement: Chunk reclamation respects the grace window, protecting against re-reference races
186: Requirement: Chunk storage is content-addressed and globally deduplicated
187: Requirement: history is newest-first, scoped per wallet+network, and supports cursor paging with no gap or duplicate
188: Requirement: manifestHash is computed once at write time from the ordered chunk-hash sequence
189: Requirement: prune retains exactly the N newest complete manifests per wallet+network
